summary_one_line: Input must carry a valid URL/scheme so a connection is cached, making the while(conn) loop non-empty at cleanup.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally well-formed URL with a recognizable scheme/host:port (e.g. literal bytes like '://1','HTTP/' and a port '9001') so curl actually opens and caches a connection; only then is conncache_find_first_connection non-NULL and the while(conn) true-side taken during close-all.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry concentrated structural tokens at fixed offsets: '://' and '9001' (0x0a..0x1e) and the 'HTTP/2' / 'GHTTP/27.0' magic at 0x24+, which the cmplog/vpc I2S stage planted by substituting CMP operands; the branch's true side is hit 332 (vpc) / 45 (cmplog) times.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short, scheme-corrupted byte runs (e.g. '%' / '+' / 'A' fill) lacking the '://' + port + HTTP-version structure at the same offsets, so no connection is cached and the loop body never runs (hitcount 13 / 1).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog and value_profile_cmplog add I2SRandReplace, which reads CMP operands from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the matching operand bytes (scheme/port/HTTP-version literals) into the input; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot place these multi-byte literals to make a connection get cached.

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs differ by the I2S technique.
The cmplog>naive pair is also an I2S delta.
The blocked branch is the while(conn) loop guard whose body removes/closes cached connections.
Loop entry depends on conncache_find_first_connection returning a connection.
The winner takes the loop body and the true side; loser never enters it.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L 539  T=17 F=17  T=0 F=20  while(conn) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner-resolving seeds carry structural URL tokens '://', port '9001' and HTTP-version literals.
Winner seed embeds an HTTP-version magic 'GHTTP/27.0'.
Winner takes the true branch far more often than the loser (332 vs 13).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=332  loser=13

evidence_trail.claim: Loser-blocking seeds are short, scheme-corrupted byte runs.
At the discriminating offsets the winner holds the URL structure ('/' at 0x0c, '9' at 0x17) while the loser holds filler bytes.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes CMP operands into the input at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets
any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 23 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 32d10b449c4075ee`; the tool reported 'I2S-floor signal: 2 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 23, 24', i.e. two ParentInfo-only (mutation_op = -) ancestors in the winning seed's chain, the floor signature of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current cmplog build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness in which the while(conn) true side is reachable without any cached connection (loop entry independent of a parsed scheme/host:port literal) so naive resolves it as often as cmplog/vpc, eliminating the I2S advantage.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact CMP operands I2S substitutes (scheme vs port vs HTTP-version) are inferred from seed byte runs rather than from per-comparison CMP logs, so which literal is the gating one is uncertain.
